questions.question: What is a circle?
questions.correct_answer: A set of all points in a plane equidistant from a fixed point called the centre
questions.explanation: The fixed point is the centre and the distance from it is the radius.

questions.question: Define radius and diameter of a circle.
questions.correct_answer: Radius is distance from centre to a point on the circle; diameter is twice the radius
questions.explanation: Diameter passes through the centre and connects two points on the circle.

questions.question: What is a chord?
questions.correct_answer: A line segment joining two points on a circle
questions.explanation: A chord may or may not pass through the centre; if it does, it is the diameter.

questions.question: State the relation between diameter and radius.
questions.explanation: Every diameter is twice as long as the radius.

questions.question: What is a tangent to a circle?
questions.correct_answer: A line that touches the circle at exactly one point
questions.explanation: The point of contact is where the tangent meets the circle; it is perpendicular to the radius at that point.

questions.question: State the properties of tangents to a circle.
questions.correct_answer: A tangent is perpendicular to the radius at the point of contact
questions.explanation: This is a fundamental property used in many problems.

questions.question: What is a secant?
questions.correct_answer: A line that intersects a circle at two points
questions.explanation: Secants pass through the circle cutting it at two distinct points.

questions.question: State the angle subtended by a semicircle.
questions.correct_answer: The angle subtended by a semicircle at any point on the circle is a right angle
questions.explanation: This is also called the Thales Theorem.

questions.question: State the relation between angles in the same segment.
questions.correct_answer: Angles subtended by the same chord at the circumference are equal
questions.explanation: This helps in solving problems involving circles and angles.

questions.question: State the relation between tangent and chord at the point of contact.
questions.correct_answer: Angle between tangent and chord = angle subtended by chord in alternate segment
questions.explanation: This is known as the Alternate Segment Theorem.

notes.title: Circle Basics
notes.content: A circle has a centre, radius, diameter, and circumference. Diameter = 2 × radius.

notes.title: Chord and Tangent
notes.content: Chord joins two points; tangent touches circle at one point perpendicular to radius.

notes.title: Angle Properties
notes.content: Angle in semicircle = 90°; angles in same segment are equal; tangent-chord angle = angle in alternate segment.

facts.fact: All radii of a circle are equal.
Diameter is the longest chord of a circle.
Tangent is perpendicular to the radius at point of contact.
Angles subtended by same chord at circumference are equal.
